title: InverseMetric
summary: Specifies inverse ambient metric components and verifies the formal block inverse identities in normal coordinates.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specifies inverse ambient metric components and verifies the formal block inverse identities in normal coordinates.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/FG/InverseMetric.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normal metric components together with a formal inverse base block. Its mathematical data are: g Inv: ι maps to ι maps to the real numbers.
correspondence.leanType: structure NormalInverseMetricComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The normal-form inverse ambient metric components g~^IJ. In explicit terms, for c of type normal inverse metric components ι, its defining expression is | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.zero maps to 0 | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.base maps to 0 | Ambient Index.zero, Ambient Index.inf maps to C.t⁻¹ | Ambient Index.base, Ambient Index.zero maps to 0 | Ambient Index.base i, Ambient Index.base j maps to C.t to the power 2 ⁻¹ times C.g Inv i j | Ambient Index.base, Ambient Index.inf maps to 0 | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.zero maps to C.t⁻¹ | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.base maps to 0 | Ambient Index.inf, Ambient Index.inf maps to -2 times C.rho times C.t to the power 2 ⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: def inverseMetricComp (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Inverse Metric Components ι, inverse Metric Comp C Ambient Index.zero Ambient Index.zero equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Inverse Metric Components ι, inverse Metric Comp C Ambient Index.zero Ambient Index.inf equals C.t⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.inf = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Inverse Metric Components ι, inverse Metric Comp C Ambient Index.inf Ambient Index.zero equals C.t⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.zero = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Inverse Metric Components ι, and i, j of type ι, inverse Metric Comp C Ambient Index.base i Ambient Index.base j equals C.t to the power 2 ⁻¹ times C.g Inv i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,C\colon \operatorname{NormalInverseMetricComponents} ι,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad \operatorname{inverseMetricComp} C (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} i) (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} j) = (t_{\mathrm{C}} ^ 2)⁻¹ \cdot C.\operatorname{gInv} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_base_base (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (i j : ι) : inverseMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹ * C.gInv i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type Normal Inverse Metric Components ι, inverse Metric Comp C Ambient Index.inf Ambient Index.inf equals -2 times C.rho times C.t to the power 2 ⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = -2 * C.rho * (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Left inverse condition for the base block. In explicit terms, for c of type normal inverse metric components ι, its defining expression is for every i j : ι, ∑ k : ι, C.g Inv i k times C.g k j equals if i equals j then 1 else 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{BaseLeftInverse}(C)=\forall i j \colon ι, (\sum k \colon ι, C.\operatorname{gInv} i k \cdot g_{\mathrm{C}} k j) = \operatorname{if} i = j \operatorname{then} 1 \operatorname{else} 0
correspondence.leanType: def BaseLeftInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Right inverse condition for the base block. In explicit terms, for c of type normal inverse metric components ι, its defining expression is for every i j : ι, ∑ k : ι, C.g i k times C.g Inv k j equals if i equals j then 1 else 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{BaseRightInverse}(C)=\forall i j \colon ι, (\sum k \colon ι, g_{\mathrm{C}} i k \cdot C.\operatorname{gInv} k j) = \operatorname{if} i = j \operatorname{then} 1 \operatorname{else} 0
correspondence.leanType: def BaseRightInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The base-base block of g~^{IK} g~_{KJ} reduces to the ordinary inverse identity for g^{ik} g_kj. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,\operatorname{Fintype} ι,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;C\colon \operatorname{NormalInverseMetricComponents} ι,\;ht\colon t_{\mathrm{C}} \ne 0,\;hbase\colon \operatorname{BaseLeftInverse} C,\;i,j\colon ι,\quad (\sum k \colon ι, \operatorname{inverseMetricComp} C (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} i) (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} k) \cdot \operatorname{ambientMetricComp} C.\operatorname{toNormalMetricComponents} (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} k) (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} j)) = \operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{kronecker} (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} i) (\operatorname{AmbientIndex}.\operatorname{base} j)
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverse_metric_base_base_left [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (ht : C.t ≠ 0) (hbase : BaseLeftInverse C) (i j : ι) : (∑ k : ι, inverseMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base k) * ambientMetricComp C.toNormalMetricComponents (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base j)) = AmbientIndex.kronecker (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j)

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
